relations.note: Known-safe brand combinations do not replace the requirement to test every text/background pair.
The gold-on-white prohibition identifies a specific failing combination under the general contrast requirement.

quotes: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.
WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.

rules.intent: Ensure every text treatment remains legible at WCAG AA contrast levels.
rules.rule_text: Test every text/background pair with WebAIM or an equivalent tool. Meet the WCAG AA body-text minimum (body.contrast.minimum = 4.5:1) and large-text minimum (large_text.contrast.minimum = 3:1); the source defines large text as at least 24px, or 19px when bold.

quotes: Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.

rules.intent: Provide prequalified brand-color combinations that support readable text.
rules.rule_text: Known-safe dark-on-light combinations are Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, or Graphite text on White, Light Blue, or Golden Sand. Known-safe reverse combinations are White text on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, or Dark Navy.

effect.reserved_for: surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis
effect.subject: tok_lisraya_color_primary_gold and tok_lisraya_color_primary_sunshine

quotes: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.
Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast)
golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent low-contrast gold text while preserving gold as a brand accent.
rules.rule_text: Do not use Gold or Sunshine as text on White because the combination fails contrast. Reserve gold colors for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

effect.b: icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.
Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Keep meaning understandable for people who cannot perceive color distinctions.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; pair color with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.
